senses.definition: the act of drawing attention to oneself by displaying showy or flamboyant behavior
examples: His constant showboating frustrated his teammates.
The coach criticized the players' showboating after scoring.

senses.definition: people who show off or perform flashy displays to attract attention
examples: The team were full of showboats who ignored the basics.
Avoid being a showboat; focus on solid play.

senses.definition: to behave or perform in a way that draws attention to oneself by showing off
examples: They love showboating for the cameras.
Don't showboat—keep your head in the game.

senses.definition: a device or container that displays or presents content publicly; also a brand/product name in some contexts
examples: The gallery used a digital showbox to display the artworks.
Our showbox includes all the promotional videos.

senses.definition: plural of showbox; containers or devices that display content publicly
examples: The exhibition used several showboxes for installations.
They repaired the showboxes before the event.

senses.definition: archaic or dialect term for bread used for a show or display; not common in modern usage
examples: The shop displayed showbread in a decorative tray.
Historically, showbread was placed in religious settings.

senses.definition: plural of showbread; bread used for display in historical or ceremonial contexts
examples: The museum featured a replica of showbreads.
Religious artifacts included showbreads on a pedestal.

senses.definition: a place or event where good things are displayed to show them off
examples: The museum opened a new showcase for contemporary art.
The gallery held a showcase of regional designers.

senses.definition: to display or present something in a way that highlights its best features
examples: They showcased their latest collection at the trade fair.
The university showcased student research projects.

senses.definition: past tense of showcase; displayed or presented to highlight its features
examples: The new model was showcased at the conference.
The designer showcased her work to potential buyers.

senses.definition: plural of showcase; events or displays where items are shown to highlight them
examples: The festival features several music showcases.
She attended a fashion showcase to see the new lines.

senses.definition: third person singular present of showcase; displays or presents something to highlight its qualities
examples: The company showcases its innovations weekly.
The program showcases local talent.

senses.definition: the act of displaying or presenting something to highlight its best features
examples: The showroom is showcasing a new line of sunglasses.
They are showcasing student work at the open house.

senses.definition: nonstandard or misspelled form of 'showed' (past tense of 'show') used informally or dialectally
examples: He showedd the document to the team (informal/typo).
She showedd interest in the proposal.

senses.definition: nonstandard or misspelled form of 'showed' or 'showed' plus extra 'd'—not a standard entry
examples: That form is likely a misspelling showded in the text.

senses.definition: nonstandard or rare form; not recognized as a standard word in major dictionaries
examples: The manuscript includes an irregular 'showding' term.

senses.definition: nonstandard or humorous coinage implying a domain of display or showiness; not a standard term
examples: He entered the world of showdom with bold confidence.

senses.definition: a decisive confrontation or final contest
examples: The two rivals met in a tense showdown.
Fans waited for the showdown between the teams.

senses.definition: plural of showdown; decisive confrontations or final contests
examples: The season concludes with several showdowns between top teams.
There were heated showdowns at the debate.

senses.definition: unlikely or erroneous sequence; not a standard word
examples: The string 'showds' appears to be a typo.

senses.definition: past tense of show; presented or demonstrated something to others
examples: She showed her notes to the teacher.
He showed us how it works.

senses.definition: a brief period of rain or a device that sprays water for washing
examples: We had a light shower this afternoon.
Take a shower after the gym.

senses.definition: to wash oneself or to bathe with a spray of water
examples: I will shower before dinner.
She showered quickly and dressed.

senses.definition: past tense of shower; washed or rained on briefly
examples: She showered quickly and left the house.
The sky showered them with light rain.

senses.definition: person who showers; one who gives showers (e.g., baby shower host)
examples: The aunt is the showerer at the baby shower.

senses.definition: plural of showerer; people who give or partake in showers
examples: Baby showerers exchanged gifts.

senses.definition: informal or humorous term meaning very wet or abundant with showers
examples: The day was showerful with rain.
A showerful of confetti greeted them at the door.

senses.definition: the part of a shower that releases water
examples: The showerhead was leaking.
Replace the showerhead for better water pressure.

senses.definition: plural of showerhead; parts that release water in showers
examples: New showerheads can save water.
The store sells various showerheads.
